titleStmt.title: Letter from the Administrative Area Office, Royal Hospital Kilmainham, to the Chief Crown Solicitor, 26 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: A colonel

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from the Administrative Area Office of the Royal Hospital Kilmainham to the Chief Crown Solicitor for Ireland, Malachy Kelly (1850-1916). The letter concerns the injury of Patrick Sheehan (1879-?), a labourer from Cork, who injured himself while working. The letter states the wages of Mr Sheehan at the time of injury, also stating he has no written contract with the employer.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/3785

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Administrative Area Officer
Please see Solicitor Generals queries opposite
and let me know full testimonials
M. Kelly
9.3.16 Ref.No.155/583 2 2.
Subject. Injury to Patk. Sheehan. A.A.O., Ireland,
Royal Hospital,
Dublin. 26th February, 1916. The Chief Crown Solicitor,
Dublin Castle, Sir, In reply to your minute of 25/2/16,
this man is a casual labourer employed at 6d per
hour as and when required. There is no written
contract as to the terms of his employment. Yours faithfully, Colonel,
Administrative Area Officer.
M.R. 2
I require to know a great deal
more about this mans employment
to be able to say if he was "employed
with course than for the purposes of
the employment trade or harriers'
what was he carrying the sacks
for? Etc. Etc.
EOF. Solicitor General
Please advise.
M. Kelly.
29.2/16
